core_principles: Ultra-lightweight and mobile-first (optimized for fast loading on 3G/4G).
Civic and trustworthy: Interface should feel like a reliable public service, not a corporate sales pitch.
Emergency clarity: Critical medical information must surface instantly with zero cognitive load.
Made in India: Subtle premium nods to Indian identity without kitsch (e.g., Ashoka Navy, Terracotta accents).

fonts.body_and_regional: IBM Plex Sans (ensures high-quality Devanagari/Hindi rendering)

rules: Do not use generic fonts like Inter or Roboto for headings.
Ensure strong tracking and legibility for medical data.
Use font-black for emergency call-to-actions to maximize visibility.
CRITICAL: The 'emergency_red' color is STRICTLY reserved for Medical/Emergency UI and the 'Call Emergency Contact' button. Never use it for decorative elements or standard cancel buttons.
Maintain WCAG AA minimum 4.5:1 contrast ratio, especially for the public Finder page.
Avoid muddy grays; tint neutral greys slightly with blue (cool).
Finder Page: Single column, stacked layout with massive touch targets (h-14 or h-16 buttons).
Dashboard: Grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3. Cardless or minimal 1px border cards. Use full height children to prevent jagged bottoms.
Medical Mode: Add generous padding (p-6 to p-8) around critical fields to prevent misreading.

buttons.primary: Solid Ashoka Navy background, white text, subtle hover lift (-translate-y-1).
buttons.emergency: Solid Signal Red background, white text, massive touch area, sticky to bottom on mobile.
buttons.quick_action: Outline buttons with muted foreground text for 'Wrong parking' or 'Headlight on'.

surfaces.health_data: Flat solid surfaces only. NO glassmorphism for medical data to ensure absolute clarity.
surfaces.owner_dashboard: Neumorphic tactile or crisp 1px borders with subtle shadow.

icons.usage: Use thick stroke-width (2.5) for emergency icons, standard (2.0) for dashboard.

motion_and_interaction.transitions: Fast and purposeful. 0.15s ease-in-out for buttons.
motion_and_interaction.loading: Pulse animations on skeleton loaders instead of spinners to feel faster on poor connections.
motion_and_interaction.emergency_trigger: Haptic feedback pattern (if supported) and immediate visual color shift.

finder_page.design: Ultra-minimalist. Logo at top, big scanning success message, huge quick action buttons, language toggle (EN/HI) prominent at top right.
finder_page.medical_mode: Screen background shifts to a subtle red tint. Massive typography for Blood Group and Allergies. Bottom 20% of screen is a fixed 'CALL EMERGENCY CONTACT' button.

owner_dashboard.design: Clean, tabular, categorized bento grid. Quick stats at top (Total Scans, Active Tags), Tabbed view for Vehicle, Pet, Luggage, Keys, Medical.
owner_dashboard.qr_generator: Print-preview style layout. Black and white high-contrast previews of A4, ID-card, and Keyring layouts.

rules: Use SVG icons over raster images to maintain the 'ultra-lightweight' strict requirement.
No heavy hero images on the Finder app; rely on typography and brand colors.

landing_hero_abstract.description: Subtle, trustworthy gradient/abstract background for the marketing landing page hero.

medical_trust.description: Medical professional or sterile environment for the Medical Disclaimer/Info page.

testing.attributes: All interactive elements MUST use data-testid attributes (e.g., data-testid='finder-call-owner-btn', data-testid='emergency-call-btn').

instructions_to_main_agent: Configure Tailwind with the provided color system. Extend the theme with 'primary', 'accent', and 'emergency' colors.
Use standard Shadcn UI for the Owner Dashboard (Tabs, Cards, Tables, Dialogs), but strip the borders to make it feel cleaner (Cardless approach).
For the public Finder Page, write raw HTML/Tailwind to keep the bundle size minimal. Do NOT use heavy client-side state if avoidable.
Implement internationalization (i18n) structure early for English and Hindi (Devanagari).
Build the QR PDF generator using standard print CSS media queries or lightweight jsPDF.
Ensure 'Made in India' is subtly placed in the footer of public pages.
